rawAdvertText:
General Manager - The Vaults - job post
Ally Consultants Ltd
35 Glebe Street, Stoke-on-Trent ST4 1HG
From £50,000 a year - Full-time
Job details
Here’s how the job details align with your profile.

Pay


From £50,000 a year


Job type


Full-time


Shift and schedule


Weekend availability

 
Location


Estimated commute
25 minutes from 32 Sandon Street

Job address
35 Glebe Street, Stoke-on-Trent ST4 1HG
 
Full job description
The Opportunity

The Vaults is about to become Stoke-on-Trent’s most exciting new destination.

Set within one of the city’s most iconic and architecturally impressive buildings — recently refurbished and brought back to life — it will bring together a standout food hall, a premium bar, and a high-energy events programme designed to draw people in, day and night.

From packed weekends to private functions and live experiences, The Vaults is being built to create atmosphere, momentum, and real commercial impact.

We are looking for a highly experienced operator to take full ownership and run this standout venue with confidence, control, and commercial focus.
This is a role for someone who doesn’t wait for direction — they take it.

This is not a desk job

This is a hands-on leadership role in a fast-moving, multi-use environment.

You will be responsible for running the entire operation — from vendors and bar performance to events , people, security, maintenance and cleaning and the building itself.

What You’ll Be Doing

Taking full control of day-to-day operations across the venue
Managing and holding independent food & drink vendors to high standards
Overseeing bar operations, stock control, and margins
Driving and delivering a strong events and private functions calendar
Managing budgets, costs, and revenue performance
Ensuring the building is safe, compliant, and well maintained
Leading, motivating, and holding staff accountable
Acting as the on-site decision maker at all times
What We’re Looking For

Proven experience running a busy hospitality or multi-use venue
Strong commercial awareness — you understand margins, costs, and performance
Experience managing multiple stakeholders or vendors
A problem solver who acts quickly and decisively
A confident leader who is not afraid to challenge poor performance
Someone who thrives in a fast-paced, high-pressure environment
What This Role Is NOT

Not a figurehead role
Not for someone who manages from behind a laptop
Not for someone who waits to be told what to do
What You’ll Get

£50,000 + performance-based bonus
The opportunity to take full ownership of a flagship venue
A fast-paced environment where your decisions have real impact
Direct access to decision-makers — no unnecessary layers
The Bottom Line

If you’re someone who:

Walks into a venue and instantly sees what’s working — and what isn’t
Takes action instead of talking about it
Sets high standards and expects others to meet them
Then this is your opportunity to build something exceptional.

Pay: From £50,000.00 per year

Work Location: In person
shortCompanyReason: Advert is posted by Ally Consultants Ltd for The Vaults venue role.
shortRoleReason: Senior hands-on general manager role running a new hospitality and events venue.
companySummary: Ally Consultants Ltd is advertising a leadership role for The Vaults, a newly refurbished venue in Stoke-on-Trent.
roleSummary: The General Manager will take full ownership of a multi-use venue combining food hall, bar, and events, with responsibility for operations, people, vendors, compliance, and commercial performance.
headlineAttraction: Full ownership of a flagship new venue with real commercial impact and performance bonus.
rolePurpose: To run the entire venue operation and drive commercial success through strong leadership, standards, and execution.
advertSummary: A full-time, in-person General Manager role for The Vaults in Stoke-on-Trent, offering from £50,000 plus bonus. The role is highly hands-on and commercially focused, leading day-to-day operations across vendors, bar, events, staff, safety, and maintenance in a fast-paced hospitality environment.
personalisedIntro: I’ve spent my career stepping into complex, fast-moving operations and making them work better. The Vaults feels like the kind of role where that matters: full ownership, live trading pressure, multiple moving parts, and a clear need for someone who can keep standards high while the venue builds momentum.
whyThisRole: This role fits the way I work best: close to the operation, accountable for outcomes, and expected to make decisions quickly. I’d be comfortable with the mix of vendors, bar performance, events, compliance, and day-to-day control because that combination is where I’ve done my strongest work.
companyPridePoints: Food hall, premium bar, and events programme
coreResponsibilities: Take full control of day-to-day operations
Manage independent food and drink vendors
Oversee bar operations, stock control, and margins
Deliver events and private functions calendar
Manage budgets, costs, and revenue performance
Ensure the building is safe, compliant, and maintained
Lead, motivate, and hold staff accountable
essentialRequirements: Proven experience running a busy hospitality or multi-use venue
Experience managing multiple stakeholders or vendors
Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, high-pressure environment
likelyBusinessNeeds: Launch and stabilise a new venue
impliedStrategicGoals: Create Stoke-on-Trent’s most exciting new destination

personalisedContent.heroPositioning: Hands-on operator who brings control, commercial grip, and calm decisions in busy venues.
personalisedContent.personalisedOpening: I’ve spent my career stepping into complex, fast-moving operations and making them work better. The Vaults feels like the kind of role where that matters: full ownership, live trading pressure, multiple moving parts, and a clear need for someone who can keep standards high while the venue builds momentum.
personalisedContent.whyThisCompany: Ally Consultants Ltd is backing a venue with real ambition behind it, and The Vaults sounds like a proper flagship project rather than a routine site. I’m drawn to the fact that it’s a newly refurbished building being positioned as a destination with food, drink, and events all under one roof.
personalisedContent.whyThisRole: This role fits the way I work best: close to the operation, accountable for outcomes, and expected to make decisions quickly. I’d be comfortable with the mix of vendors, bar performance, events, compliance, and day-to-day control because that combination is where I’ve done my strongest work.

selectedEvidenceExamples.bestMatchedRoleNeed: running a busy operation with control under pressure
selectedEvidenceExamples.whyChosen: I led day-to-day operations in a scale-up environment where discipline, resilience, and clear execution mattered. That translates well to a venue that needs strong grip across multiple moving parts.
selectedEvidenceExamples.suggestedUsage: Use as the main operational proof point for hands-on leadership and control.
selectedEvidenceExamples.shortLine: I’m used to keeping a busy operation steady while the pressure is on.

selectedEvidenceExamples.bestMatchedRoleNeed: commercial awareness, budgets, costs, and revenue performance
selectedEvidenceExamples.whyChosen: I’ve helped improve profit, working capital, and cash performance in a regulated healthcare setting. That gives me a practical commercial mindset, not just an operational one.
selectedEvidenceExamples.suggestedUsage: Use as the strongest commercial evidence on the page.
selectedEvidenceExamples.shortLine: I pay attention to margin, cash, and the numbers behind the operation.

selectedEvidenceExamples.exampleTitle: Ride compliance and operator progression tracking
selectedEvidenceExamples.bestMatchedRoleNeed: safety, compliance, and standards in a live environment
selectedEvidenceExamples.whyChosen: I built a practical tracking system in a safety-critical leisure environment to support compliance and operator progression. That is relevant to a venue where standards, safety, and accountability cannot slip.
selectedEvidenceExamples.suggestedUsage: Use as supporting proof for compliance and standards.
selectedEvidenceExamples.shortLine: I’m comfortable putting simple systems in place when safety and consistency matter.

personalisedContent.roleNeedsSummary: My read is that this role needs someone who can run the venue properly, not just oversee it. It needs commercial judgement, fast decisions, and enough presence to hold vendors, staff, and standards together in a live environment.

experienceMappings.myEvidence: I led day-to-day operations in a specialist manufacturing business during a scale-up phase, where I had to keep delivery disciplined and the operation resilient.
experienceMappings.relevance: Shows I can take responsibility for a live operation and keep it moving.

experienceMappings.myEvidence: I supported a £149.9k improvement in profit before appropriation, released £33.3k of working capital, and helped increase cash at bank by £172.6k year on year.
experienceMappings.relevance: Direct evidence that I understand the financial side of running an operation.

experienceMappings.myEvidence: I’ve managed and supported a team through business-critical projects in a high-security environment, balancing service standards, workload, and delivery pressure.
experienceMappings.relevance: Shows I can coordinate people and keep standards up when there are multiple demands.

experienceMappings.myEvidence: I created a user input system to monitor ride operating hours and support compliance and operator progression in a safety-critical leisure setting.
experienceMappings.relevance: Relevant to a venue where compliance and accountability need to be visible and practical.

experienceMappings.myEvidence: In scale-up conditions, I had to improve processes, equipment, capacity, and ways of working to support growth and resilience.
experienceMappings.relevance: Matches the need for someone who acts quickly when the operation needs fixing.

focusAreasToBring.title: Operational grip
focusAreasToBring.summary: I would focus on keeping the venue stable, visible, and well controlled so issues are dealt with early rather than left to build.

focusAreasToBring.title: Commercial discipline
focusAreasToBring.summary: I would keep a close eye on margins, costs, and revenue performance so decisions are grounded in what the venue is actually delivering.

focusAreasToBring.title: Standards across multiple operators
focusAreasToBring.summary: I would make expectations clear and hold people to them, especially where independent vendors and live service standards need to stay aligned.

personalisedContent.fitSummary: I bring a mix of hands-on operations, commercial control, and experience in environments where standards matter. I’ve worked across leisure, healthcare, manufacturing, and regulated services, so I’m comfortable with complexity, pace, and accountability. That makes me a strong fit for a venue that needs someone to run the operation properly and keep it commercially sharp.
personalisedContent.likelyContributionSummary: Based on what the brief describes, I’d expect to spend the first months getting tight control of the operation, understanding the moving parts, and making sure the basics are solid. I’d also look to build confidence around vendor standards, stock and margin control, and the rhythm of events so the venue can trade well and stay consistent.
companyHighlights: The Vaults is being positioned as a newly refurbished venue in Stoke-on-Trent.
The brief describes a food hall, premium bar, and events programme in one venue.
The role offers full ownership and direct access to decision-makers.
personalisedContent.cultureFitSummary: I work best in environments where I’m expected to be present, make decisions, and deal with issues directly. That fits the hands-on, no-layers style described in the advert.

first90DaysPlan.focus: Get under the skin of the operation
first90DaysPlan.detail: I would spend time understanding the venue, the trading rhythm, the people, the vendors, the compliance position, and the current commercial picture so I can see where control is strong and where it needs tightening.
I would focus on stock, margins, budgets, standards, and accountability, making sure the key operational measures are clear and that issues are being tracked and acted on quickly.
I would work on strengthening the events calendar, sharpening vendor performance, and building a more consistent operating rhythm that supports atmosphere, standards, and revenue.

personalisedContent.closingSummary: I’d bring the kind of hands-on leadership that keeps a busy venue controlled, commercial, and ready to perform.
closingProofPoints: I’ve improved profit, cash, and working capital in a live operation.
I’ve led scale-up delivery in a fast-moving specialist environment.
I’ve built practical compliance and reporting tools where standards mattered.
contentNotes: I’ve kept the evidence focused on operations, commercial control, compliance, and live-environment leadership because those are the strongest matches to the brief.
I haven’t added company facts beyond the advert.

examples.title: “Built live operational dashboard concept for GP practice visibility”
